methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
CarbonCapture Inc. is a Series B climate & energy company founded by Adrian Corless, currently valued at $300M post-money with $80M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public (LNZA) and eight private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and Cemvita Factory.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields an implied valuation of $570M from the current $300M base. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $400M, with the sample range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The peer cohort's broad dispersion reflects variance in technology maturity and market positioning within frontier energy.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a valuation range of $340M to $656M, with a median of $485M. The current post-money valuation of $300M sits below this implied range, suggesting modest upside potential contingent on execution. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among peers—particularly the presence of $5B+ outliers—and the early-stage nature of technology commercialization in climate solutions, which affects comparability and risk assessment across the peer set.
